mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 1.5% signal aggressive monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed emergency rate cuts in March 2020
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window post-trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions cascade through equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or correlation between UNRATE changes and subsequent 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month signals demand destruction that reduces near-term corporate revenue and profit margins. Combined with rising unemployment, this dual demand shock produces negative 20-day equity returns as forward earnings estimates are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 5% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in same month
regime_hint: demand shock with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (measured as deviation from additive single-series effects) is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines below 102.0 signals supply-side contraction and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization. In a demand-shock regime, this supply weakness amplifies earnings pressure and produces negative 5-day returns as market reprices growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO < 102.0, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 6-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) declines month-over-month by more than 500 thousand persons signals acute labor market shock that triggers flight-to-safety equity selling and negative 20-day returns as investors reprrice recession probability and equity risk premiums expand.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the magnitude of negative returns does not correlate significantly with magnitude of payroll decline (correlation < 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1200 thousand units combined with retail sales decline signals broad-based demand destruction across residential and consumer sectors. This dual demand weakness produces negative 60-day equity returns as forward GDP growth expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1200 thousand AND RSAFS declines > 3% month-over-month
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction across housing and consumption
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (HOUST × RSAFS) explains less than 5% additional variance beyond single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 2.5% year-over-year while federal funds rate is cut below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation without inflation constraint. This policy space enables sustained equity support, producing positive 60-day returns as real discount rates compress and growth expectations stabilize.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the policy accommodation signal (measured as FEDFUNDS minus inflation expectations) does not predict positive returns with Rank IC > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) contracts quarter-over-quarter while unemployment rises above 4.0% signals recession entry that triggers equity repricing downward. This dual recession signal produces negative 20-day returns as forward earnings estimates are revised sharply lower and equity risk premiums expand.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines quarter-over-quarter AND UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in same quarter
regime_hint: recession entry with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the recession signal (joint GDPC1 and UNRATE deterioration) does not predict negative returns with Rank IC > 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
